evals.name: ecib-pre-disbursement-mfb
evals.prompt: Is a pre-disbursement eCIB check mandatory for a Microfinance Bank making a Rs. 50,000 consumer loan? Cite the exact circular and section.

assertions.text: Answer states YES (pre-disbursement eCIB check is mandatory)
Cites [[SBP_eCIB_Master]] doc_id wikilink
Includes #page= deep link to the source PDF
Includes a chunk_id like SBP_eCIB_Master::p
Does not fabricate a section number outside the actual document

evals.name: contact-list-prohibition
evals.prompt: Can a SECP-licensed digital lending NBFC ask the user for permission to access their phone contacts to improve credit decisions? Be specific about which regulation answers this.

assertions.text: Answer states NO — prohibition is absolute and overrides user consent
Cites at least one of SECP_8_2024, SECP_10_2023, SECP_12_2024
Mentions that consent does not override the prohibition
assertions.check: contains_any:irrespective of consent,overrides user consent,even if the user consents,regardless of consent

assertions.text: Provides a clickable source URL

evals.name: validate-bad-prd
evals.prompt:
Review this PRD for any Pakistan regulatory issues:

---
Product: NanoCash — instant 14-day digital loans up to Rs. 25,000 for salaried workers.
Lender: NanoCash Lending Limited (SECP-licensed NBFC, NBMFC category).
Channel: Mobile app.
Onboarding: User opens app, provides CNIC, takes a selfie. We hit NADRA Verisys + BVS. Onboarding completes in 90 seconds.
Underwriting signals: NADRA data + phone metadata + user's contact list (with explicit consent flag at install time) + SMS read (with consent) + location.
Fees: 5% flat origination fee + 2% per week markup. APR works out to ~120%.
Disclosure: We display total amount payable on the disbursement screen before user taps 'Borrow'.
Collections: If overdue 3 days, our bot calls the user. If 7 days, the bot calls 3 numbers from the user's contact list. If 14 days, third-party agency (Quick Recovery Services Pvt Ltd) takes over.
Repayment: Easypaisa, JazzCash, bank transfer.
Licensing: We are listed on SECP's whitelist as of last quarter.
---

Return the structured verdict with BLOCKERS, WARNINGS and INFO. Cite every finding.

assertions.text: Flags contact-list access as a BLOCKER citing SECP digital-lending circulars
Flags calling 3 numbers from contact list as a BLOCKER (fair-debt-collection)
Flags absence of KFS before disbursement as a BLOCKER
Flags collection at 3 days as too aggressive (14-day minimum notice rule)
Flags APR/tenor/fee structure under Circular 10/2023 nano-lending caps
Flags missing pre-disbursement eCIB / private bureau check
Uses BLOCKER / WARNING / INFO severity structure
Cites at least 3 doc_id wikilinks

evals.name: validate-emi-with-lending
evals.prompt:
Review this idea for regulatory issues:

We're an SBP-licensed EMI running a digital wallet (PakWallet). We have 2M active users. We want to launch 'Pay-Later for Vendors' — buy now, pay merchants in 30 days. We'd advance the merchant immediately from our customer-funds account, and collect from the customer at month-end. No interest, just a 1.5% fee. Customer signs a digital agreement.

Is this allowed?

assertions.text: Identifies EMIs cannot lend as a primary BLOCKER
assertions.check: contains_any:EMIs shall not,EMI cannot lend,EMIs cannot lend,EMIs are prohibited,EMIs may not lend,not conduct the business of banking,prohibited from lending

assertions.text: Flags use of customer-funds trust account for non-permitted purpose
Cites EMI Regulations doc_id
Suggests legitimate paths (NBFC license, bank partnership, sandbox)

evals.name: cite-fair-debt-14-day
evals.prompt: Give me a publication-quality citation for the rule that says banks must give the customer 14 days' notice before visiting their workplace or residence to collect on an overdue loan. I need both inline and block formats — going into a Confluence compliance page.

assertions.text: Cites [[SBP_FairDebt_2008]] doc_id
Includes BPRD Circular No. 13 of 2008 reference
Provides both Variant A (inline) and Variant B (block)
Includes verbatim quote about 14-day notice
Has a chunk_id like DOC::pN::cN or DOC::html::cN or DOC::summary
Includes a source URL (page deep-link if PDF, else plain URL with HTML-source note)

evals.name: cite-refuse-fabrication
evals.prompt: Give me a citation for the SBP circular that bans use of generative AI for credit underwriting in Pakistan.

assertions.text: Refuses to fabricate a citation for a non-existent rule
assertions.check: contains_any:cannot produce,refus,does not contain,no such,not found,cannot find,not present,does not exist,citation refused,unable to cite,fabricat

assertions.text: Does NOT invent a fake circular number or doc_id
Suggests adjacent / closest available regulations (tech-risk, model governance)
